title: Steering Generation Toward a Condition
concept: When generating from a condition (like a text prompt), we want the output to strongly match that condition, not just be a generic plausible image.

exercises.prompt: You ask an AI to draw 'a red bicycle.' What would be a DISAPPOINTING result?

options.text: A clear picture of a red bicycle
A plain blue car, nothing like a bicycle
A red bicycle leaning against a wall

exercises.explain: If you asked for a red bicycle but got a blue car, the AI ignored your condition! It made something plausible-looking, but not what you asked for.

cards.title: Not Just Any Picture — THIS Picture
cards.body: Imagine asking an artist to paint 'a dragon flying over mountains at sunset' and they hand you a nice painting of a cat sleeping on a couch. Technically it's a good painting... but it's NOT what you asked for! AI generators face this exact challenge: how do we make sure the output actually follows our instructions?

cards.title: Steering Generation
cards.body: When an AI generates something FROM a condition (like a text prompt, a sketch, or a label), we want the result to strongly match that condition — not just be some random believable output. 'Steering' means guiding the AI's generation so it pays close attention to what we actually asked for.

cards.title: GPS vs. Just Driving
cards.body: Generating without steering is like a car driving around randomly — it might end up somewhere nice, but not where you wanted. Steering generation is like turning on GPS navigation: now the car (the AI) actively follows directions to reach YOUR destination, not just any destination.

cards.title: Prompt: 'A Cat Wearing a Wizard Hat'
cards.body: A well-steered AI generates an image where a cat is clearly wearing a pointy wizard hat with stars on it. A poorly-steered AI might just generate a normal cat, or a hat floating with no cat — it 'got creative' but ignored the actual condition it was given.

cards.title: Myth: 'Any realistic-looking output is a good output.'
cards.body: Not true! An image can look perfectly realistic and still be a total FAIL if it doesn't match the condition. A gorgeous photo of a beach is a bad result if you asked for 'a snowy mountain cabin.' Matching the condition matters just as much as looking realistic.

cards.title: Generic vs. Steered Generation
cards.body: Compare what happens when a model ignores steering versus when it strongly follows the condition given to it.

left.text: Prompt: 'orange elephant' → outputs a plain gray elephant. Realistic, but ignores the color condition entirely.

right.text: Prompt: 'orange elephant' → outputs a vividly orange elephant. Realistic AND matches the exact condition asked for.

exercises.prompt: What does it mean to 'steer' generation toward a condition?

options.text: Make the AI generate completely random outputs
Guide the AI so its output strongly matches the given condition
Make the output as blurry as possible
Ignore the prompt and generate anything realistic

exercises.explain: Steering means actively guiding generation so the result truly reflects the condition (like a prompt), not just producing something generic.

exercises.prompt: If an image looks realistic and high-quality, it's automatically a good result, even if it ignores the text prompt.
exercises.explain: False! Quality isn't enough — the output must also match the condition it was given. A beautiful image that ignores the prompt is still a failure.

exercises.sentence: Steering generation means guiding the AI so its output strongly ___ the given condition.
exercises.explain: The whole point of steering is making sure the generated output truly matches the condition, like a prompt or label, instead of drifting away from it.

exercises.prompt: You prompt an AI with 'a purple house on a hill.' Which result shows GOOD steering?

options.text: A gray house in a flat field
A purple house sitting clearly on a hill
A purple car parked in a garage

exercises.explain: Good steering means the output actually reflects every part of the condition: the color (purple), the object (house), and the setting (on a hill).

exercises.prompt: An AI is asked to generate 'a small green robot holding a balloon.' It produces a photorealistic image of a large yellow truck. What is the BEST description of this result?

options.text: A great result, because it looks very realistic
A poor result, because it completely ignores the given condition
A great result, because trucks are more interesting than robots
It doesn't matter, since any generated image is correct

exercises.explain: Even though the truck image might look realistic, it fails the main goal: matching the condition. Steering generation is about making sure the output truly reflects what was asked for — here, a small green robot holding a balloon.
